posts.text:
When @AymanOghanna’s neighbour was arrested for spying he had to investigate. “I wanted to understand how the man who quietly watered his tomatoes downstairs could have been living a double life.”

Listen to “The Weekend Intelligence” for the full story https://www.economist.com/podcasts/2026/05/30/the-spy-who-lived-downstairs?taid=6a1c06a916ca9c0001d5b2de&utm_campaign=trueanthem&utm_medium=social&utm_source=twitter
posts.author: TheEconomist

posts.text:
Consider the fractal nature of the human lungs.   Then consider how we will soon innovate new products by nano engineering, 3d printing, while using novel nano-materials.  

We are on the cusp of making monumental gains in efficiency and the core technology is turbostratic fractal graphene.  It’s the structural scaffolding of our future nano tech world
posts.author: BambroughKevin
